properties.media:imagetext:
Rev. 06/12
UNITED
SAFETY
B787-8
If you are sitting in an exit row please identify yourself to a crewmember to allow for reseating if:
You lack the ability to read, speak, or understand the language, or the graphic form, or the ability
to understand oral crew commands in the language specified by the airline.
Seguridad Por favor identifiquese con un tripulante para ser cambiado de asiento si usted
está sentado en una de las hileras de la salida, y si tiene problemas para: leer, entender el idioma,
instrucciones graficas o la dificultad para entender ordenes verbales de la tripulación en el
idioma utilizado por la aerolínea.
Sécurité Si vous êtes assis sur une rangée située près d'une sortie veuillez vous identifier
à un membre de l'équipage afin que l'on puisse vous attribue un autre siège si: vous ne pouvez
pas lire, parler ou comprendre l'anglais, ou les illustrations graphiques, ou bien si vous ne
pouvez pas comprendre les instructions orales de l'equipage.
Sicherheit Wenn Sie in einer Reihe von Sitzen neben einem Notausgang sitzen, müssen
Sie die Flugzeugbesatzung verständigen, damit diese Ihnen einen neuen Platz zutellen kann,
sollten Sie: die Sprache nicht sprechen, lesen oder verstehen können, oder die grafischen
Darstellungen nicht verstehen, oder soliten Sie die mündlichen Anordnungen der
Flugzeugbesatzung nicht verstehen.
ENDNCSC MARDBRCCHDRMRDTEEM CE3EC.
Federal Regulations (FAR 121.571 & 121.577) 5) Lacks sufficient aural capacity to hear and understand
Require All Passengers:
instructions shouted by flight attendants without assistance
beyond a hearing aid;
• To review this card for their safety, regardless of 6) Lacks the ability to adequately impart information orally to
where they are sitting or their physical condition;
• To comply with these instructions, the instructions of 7) Has: a) a condition or responsiblties, such as caring for
small children,
that might prevent the person from
our crew or authorized employees, and all posted
performing one or more of the applicable functions; or b) a
and lighted signs.
condition that might cause the person harm if he or she
Selection Criteria: U.S. Federal
performs one or more of the applicable functions;
Aviation Administration (FAA) regulations
8) Requires use of a portable oxygen concentrator (POC), or;
procedures that U.S. air carriers must follow concerning exit
9) Is traveling with a pet container, service animal or emotional
row seating. No air carrier may seat a person in an exit seat if
it is determined that it is likely that the person would be unable
Functions to
be performed: In the event of an
to perform one or more of the applicable functions listed below
emergency in which a crewmember is not available to assist, a
or depicted on this safety card because the person:
passenger occupying an exit row seat may be called upon to
1) Lacks sufficient mobility, strength, or dexterity in both arms
perform the following functions:
and hands, and both legs to: a) reach upward, sideways,
1) Locate the emergency exit;
and downward to the location of emergency exit and exit-
2) Recognize the emergency exit opening mechanism;
slide operating mechanisms; b) grasp and push, pull, turn,
3) Comprehend the instructions for operating the emergency exit;
or otherwise manipulate those mechanisms; c) push,
4) Operate the emergency exit;
shove, pull, or otherwise open emergency exits; d) lift out,
5) Assess whether opening the emergency exit will increase
hold, deposit on nearby seats, or maneuver over the
hazards to which passengers may be exposed;
seatbacks to the next row objects the size and weight of
6) Follow oral directions and hand signals given by a crewmember;
over-wing window exit doors; e) remove obstructions similar
7) Stow or secure the emergency exit door so that it will not
in size and weight to over-wing exit doors; f) reach the
emergency exit expeditiously; g) maintain balance while
removing obstructions; h) exit expeditiously; i) stabilize an
escape slide after deployment, or; j) assist others in getting
off an escape slide;
2) Is less than 15 years of age or lacks the capacity to perform
8) Assess the condition of an escape slide, activate the slide,
and stabilize the slide after deployment to assist others in
getting off the slide;
9) Pass expeditiously through the emergency exit, and;
10) Assess, select, and follow a safe path away from the
one or more of the applicable functions without the
assistance of an adult companion, parent, or other relative;
3) Lacks the ability to read and understand instructions related to
emergency evacuation provided by the air carrier in printed or
graphic form or the ability to understand oral crew commands;
4) Lacks sufficient visual capacity to perform one or more of
the applicable functions without the assistance of visual
aids beyond contact lenses or eyeglasses;
The passenger must identify himself or herself to allow
reseating if he or she: a) Cannot meet the selection criteria
listed above; b) Has no discernible condition that will prevent
him or her from performing the applicable functions; c) May
suffer bodily harm as the result of performing one or more of
those functions; or d) Does not wish to perform those functions
(no reason needs to be given).
When finished reviewing this card please return it to the seat pocket for our next customer.
